verses.text: Isaiah sings of his beloved's vineyard, planted on a fruitful hill with the choicest vine, walled and pressed — yet it 'brought forth wild grapes.' God did everything for Israel. Despite every care, they yielded only worthless fruit.

Isaiah sings of his beloved's vineyard, planted on a fruitful hill with the choicest vine, walled and pressed — yet it 'brought forth wild grapes.' God did everything for Israel. Despite every care, they yielded only worthless fruit.
God asks Judah to judge between Him and His vineyard: 'what could have been done more... that I have not done?' He has spared no kindness. Their barrenness is wholly their own fault, not His neglect.
God asks Judah to judge between Him and His vineyard: 'what could have been done more... that I have not done?' He has spared no kindness. Their barrenness is wholly their own fault, not His neglect.

So He will break down its hedge and lay it waste; 'the vineyard of the LORD of hosts is the house of Israel,' from whom He looked for judgment but found oppression. The parable is explained. God's care, met with wickedness, brings the vineyard's ruin.

So He will break down its hedge and lay it waste; 'the vineyard of the LORD of hosts is the house of Israel,' from whom He looked for judgment but found oppression. The parable is explained. God's care, met with wickedness, brings the vineyard's ruin.

So He will break down its hedge and lay it waste; 'the vineyard of the LORD of hosts is the house of Israel,' from whom He looked for judgment but found oppression. The parable is explained. God's care, met with wickedness, brings the vineyard's ruin.
Woe to the greedy who 'join house to house,' until they dwell alone in the land; their estates shall be desolate. Covetous land-grabbing is cursed. What is hoarded by oppression God will leave empty.
Woe to the greedy who 'join house to house,' until they dwell alone in the land; their estates shall be desolate. Covetous land-grabbing is cursed. What is hoarded by oppression God will leave empty.
Woe to the greedy who 'join house to house,' until they dwell alone in the land; their estates shall be desolate. Covetous land-grabbing is cursed. What is hoarded by oppression God will leave empty.
Woe to those who rise early to chase strong drink and feast, who 'regard not the work of the LORD.' Pleasure-seeking blinds them to God. A life of indulgence has no eye for the hand of God.
Woe to those who rise early to chase strong drink and feast, who 'regard not the work of the LORD.' Pleasure-seeking blinds them to God. A life of indulgence has no eye for the hand of God.

Therefore His people go into captivity for want of knowledge, and the grave opens wide for their multitude; the proud are brought low and the LORD exalted in judgment. Ignorance of God leads to ruin. His holiness shines in the humbling of the proud.

Woe to those who drag sin along, who 'call evil good, and good evil,' and are 'wise in their own eyes.' They invert all morality. To rename evil as good and trust one's own wisdom is a deadly self-deceit.

Woe to the heroes at drinking who acquit the guilty for a bribe; therefore God's anger is kindled and His hand stretched out. Justice is sold for wine and money. The corrupt courts of the land provoke His wrath.

He lifts an ensign to a far nation, which comes swiftly, roaring like the sea, none weary, to seize the prey. Judgment marches in like a flood. God summons distant armies to execute the sentence on His people.
